content_markdown:
A satellite at the surface of the Earth has a weight $W$ and gravitational potential energy $E_p$. The satellite is then placed in a circular orbit with a radius twice that of the Earth.

What is the weight of the satellite and the gravitational potential energy of the satellite when placed in orbit?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing four options (A, B, C, D) for the values of Weight and Gravitational potential energy.

options.label: A
options.text: Weight: $0.25 W$; Gravitational potential energy: $0.25 E_p$

options.label: B
options.text: Weight: $0.5 W$; Gravitational potential energy: $0.25 E_p$

options.label: C
options.text: Weight: $0.25 W$; Gravitational potential energy: $0.5 E_p$

options.label: D
options.text: Weight: $0.5 W$; Gravitational potential energy: $0.5 E_p$

vibe_explanation: Weight (gravitational force) follows an inverse-square law $F \propto \frac{1}{r^2}$, so doubling the radius from $R$ to $2R$ reduces weight to $\frac{1}{2^2} = 0.25W$. Gravitational potential energy follows an inverse relationship $E_p \propto -\frac{1}{r}$, so doubling the radius reduces the magnitude to $\frac{1}{2} = 0.5E_p$.
